rulings.Case Title: CHARLES LI VS. MEI LING FANG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Airbnb Inc To Comply With Subpoena For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, April 18, 2025 Line 8. PLAINTIFF CHARLES LI's Motion To Compel Airbnb Inc To Comply With Subpoena For Production Of Documents. Plaintiff Charles Li's unopposed Motion To Compel Airbnb Inc To Comply With Subpoena For Production Of Documents is DENIED. The proof of service does not show that the moving papers were served on all parties to the action. In fact, the proof of service attached to Plaintiff's MPA shows service of a different document unrelated to this motion. The record indicates Plaintiff's personal service on AirBNB did not provide 16 court days notice of his motion. (See Rules of Court, rule 3.1346 ["A written notice and all moving papers supporting a motion to compel an answer to a deposition question or to compel production of a document or tangible thing from a nonparty deponent must be personally served on the nonparty deponent unless the nonparty deponent agrees to accept service by mail or electronic service at an address or electronic service address specified on the deposition record."]; Code Civil Procedure section 1005(b) ["Unless otherwise ordered or specifically provided by law, all moving and supporting papers shall be served and filed at least 16 court days before the hearing."].) Thus, the merits of the motion are not properly before the court. Plaintiff is ordered to submit a proposed order that includes the above text no later than 8:30 am on April 18, 2025. Plaintiff shall send the proposed order by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org. Plaintiff is ordered to serve notice of entry of any order on this motion on all parties. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: HIGH POINT FINANCE LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. VIOLIE D. PASTOR, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS TRUSTEE OF ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion For Terminating Sanction
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, April 18, 2025 Line 10. CROSS COMPLAINANT VIOLIE D. PASTOR's Motion For Terminating Sanction. Defendant Violie D. Pastor's motion for terminating sanctions is DENIED. Defendant's motion seeks terminating sanctions because cross-defendant Ta & To Holdings L.P. allegedly failed to comply with this court's February 27, 2025, order and provide discovery responses. The predicate for the motion is false as Cross-defendant provided the responses on March 18, 2025. (Daniel Decl., par. 4.) Despite receiving the responses and meeting and conferring with cross-defendant, Defendant kept this outdated motion on calendar. Defendant then argues in reply that the responses are insufficient, an issue that Cross-defendant has not had a chance to address. Defendant fails to show a willful violation. Defendant did not act with substantial justification. (Lang v. Hochman (2000) 77 Cal.App.4th 1225, 1244.) Defendant and counsel, jointly and severally, shall pay $250 as sanctions, payable to the court by way of the clerk of the court (a copy of this order to accompany payment), payment no later than by April 28, 2025, receipt filed no later than May 4, 2025. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: FABIOLA SALMERON REYES ET AL VS. INTEGRATED RESOURCES GROUP, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Amend Answer
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, April 18, 2025 Line 7. DEFENDANT RAPHAEL STONE CA, INC. FKA CALIFORNIA QUARTZ, INC.'s Motion For Leave To Amend Answer. Defendants' (RAPHAEL STONE CA, INC. FKA CALIFORNIA QUARTZ, INC. AKA CALIFORNIA-QUARTZ) motion for leave to file an amended answer is granted. Courts liberally allow amendments to answers. (Hulsey v. Koehler (1990) 218 Cal.App.3d 1150, 1159 ["In particular, liberality should be displayed in allowing amendments to answers, for a defendant denied leave to amend is permanently deprived of a defense."].) Defense counsel recently discovered that RAPHAEL STONE CA, INC. and CALIFORNIA QUARTZ, INC. are distinct entities. (Guan Decl., Exs. 1 and 2.) Therefore, it is appropriate for them to file distinct answers. There is no prejudice to Plaintiff. Plaintiff's concern that CALIFORNIA-QUARTZ will "disappear" lacks merit since it has in fact filed its own answer on March 20, 2025. RAPHAEL STONE CA, INC. has leave to file the proposed amended answer, which removes reference to the separate entity and inapposite asbestos-related statutes. The court expresses no opinion regarding the answer filed on March 20, 2025, that only references "CALIFORNIA QUARTZ" and not CALIFORNIA QUARTZ, INC. Plaintiff's request for fees/costs is denied. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)
